# Project Language & Framework

Primary Language/Framework: typescript
.cursor/ignore-verification.md:
# Ignore Verification Checklist

## Verification Checklist

- Confirm .cursorignore contains secrets, private keys, and sensitive artifacts.
- Confirm .cursorindexingignore excludes large build and dependency directories.
- Verify .gitignore interaction for files that should not be indexed.
- Check Cursor indexing counts or workspace reports after updating ignore files.
- Open a known excluded file and confirm it is not included in automatic AI context.
- For manual-only rules, verify that they can be invoked explicitly by name.

# Large directories that slow indexing

dist/
build/
__pycache__/
.venv/

# Logs
*.log
logs/
.github/copilot-instructions.md:
# Repository-Wide Custom Instructions

## Coding Standards & Conventions

- Validate all external input at system boundaries. Never trust data from users, APIs, or files without validation.
- Never hardcode credentials, API keys, tokens, or secrets. Use environment variables or secure vaults.
- Handle all errors explicitly. Never silently swallow exceptions or return undefined.
- Write descriptive, intention-revealing names for variables, functions, classes, and types.
- Use type annotations wherever they improve clarity and catch errors early.
- Keep functions small and focused. Each should do one thing well.
- Prefer existing project patterns over introducing new abstractions. Consistency beats novelty.
- Review every suggestion critically; do not accept something you cannot explain.
- Always run tests before committing. Never skip the test suite.

## Security Baseline

- Never commit secrets, API keys, tokens, or credentials to source control.
- Validate all external inputs before use.
- Follow the principle of least privilege for access and permissions.
- Prefer safe defaults over convenience in security-sensitive code.

## Protected Paths

- Do not modify generated, vendored, or third-party files.
- Do not edit lock files (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, Pipfile.lock, go.sum) by hand.
- Treat deployment manifests and binary artifacts as protected unless the task explicitly requires it.

## Stable Workflow Guidance

- Review suggestions critically and refuse anything that does not match project conventions.
- Keep changes small and incremental; prefer focused pull requests.
- Use tests to verify behavior before accepting changes.
- Validate security-sensitive code carefully, including auth, input validation, and secrets handling.
.github/copilot/settings.json: Always run tests before committing. Review suggestions critically and validate changes against project conventions.

CLAUDE.md:
# Claude Code Instructions (CLAUDE.md)

**Project Maturity:** established

## Coding Conventions

- Prefer existing patterns over introducing new abstractions — consistency over novelty.
- Keep changes small and targeted to the stated task. Do not refactor code that was not part of the request.
- Each function or module should have a single clear responsibility.
- Use descriptive, intention-revealing names for variables, functions, and types.
- Prefer explicit types and clear interfaces at public boundaries.

## Development Workflow

- Read and understand the existing code before making changes.
- Run lint and tests before finalising any change. Fix all failures — never skip or disable tests to make a change pass.
- Review every change for security implications: validate inputs, check authentication, and ensure no secrets enter source control.
- Keep changes minimal and targeted to the stated task. Do not introduce unrequested features or refactors.
- Write clear commit messages that explain why, not just what.

## Additional Guidance

- Do not modify generated, vendored, or third-party files unless the task explicitly requires it.
- Do not edit lock files (package-lock.json, yarn.lock, Pipfile.lock, go.sum) by hand.
- Treat generated directories, vendor code, and deployment manifests as protected areas.
- Keep repository boundaries clear: changes should only touch the intended module or service.
